issue_title: LinkedIn as Identity provider not working
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have searched existing issues
- [X] I have reproduced the issue with the [latest nightly release](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/releases/tag/nightly)

### Area

oidc

### Describe the bug

When using for the first time LinkedIn as an Identity Provider, Keycloak sends by default the "r_emailaddress" oAuth2 scope, to which LinkedIn responds : 

> error : unauthorized_scope_error
> error_description = "r_emailaddress" is not authorized for your application

The issue seems to be at : https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/1d444ff86228b741f85fd68aacf6e06e9d53031d/services/src/main/java/org/keycloak/social/linkedin/LinkedInIdentityProvider.java#L58

```
		// email scope is mandatory in order to resolve the username using the email address
		if (!config.getDefaultScope().contains(EMAIL_SCOPE)) {
			config.setDefaultScope(config.getDefaultScope() + " " + EMAIL_SCOPE);
		}
```

### Version

22.0.0

### Expected behavior

Keycloak should only send the requested scopes when dealing with LinkedIn (in my case, openid, profile and email).

### Actual behavior

When calling LinkedIn, keycloak always add the "r_emailaddress" scope that LinkedIn doesn't accept.

### How to Reproduce?

- Set up a new app in LinkedIn
- Request access to the "Sign In with LinkedIn using OpenID Connect" product
- Create a new LinkedIn IdP in keycloak, using the clientId and secretId from LinkedIn
- Copy the keycloak redirect URL to the LinkedIn app
- Try to login

### Anything else?

_No response_
commit_message:
Add a new identity provider for LinkedIn based on OIDC
Closes https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/issues/22383

issue_title: Email sending fails when SPI truststore is configured and hostnameVerification set to 'ANY'
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have searched existing issues
- [X] I have reproduced the issue with the [latest nightly release](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/releases/tag/nightly)

### Area

dist/quarkus

### Describe the bug

With the current Keycloak version, any mail sending fails for us with the below error, indicating that hostname verification is indeed active, even if no verification is wanted:
```
"exception":{"refId":1,"exceptionType":"jakarta.mail.MessagingException",
    "message":"Could not convert socket to TLS","frames":
    [{"class":"org.eclipse.angus.mail.smtp.SMTPTransport","method":"startTLS","line":2188},
    {"class":"org.eclipse.angus.mail.smtp.SMTPTransport","method":"protocolConnect","line":765},
...
org.keycloak.services.resources.admin.RealmAdminResource: Failed to send email 
jakarta.mail.MessagingException: Could not convert socket to TLS;
nested exception is:
    java.io.IOException: Can't verify identity of server: our-mail-server.com
```

We have Keycloak set up with the following properties setup in keycloak.conf - especially, no hostname verification :)
```yaml
# Truststore
spi-truststore-file-enabled=true
spi-truststore-file-hostname-verification-policy=ANY
spi-truststore-file-password=very-secret
spi-truststore-file-file=/path/to/keycloak/truststore.jks
```

Emails are sent using StartTLS, the realm configuration is as follows:
```json
"smtpServer": {
    "starttls": "true",
    "port": "25",
    "auth": "",
    "host": "our-mail-server.com",
    "from": "we@domain.com",
    "fromDisplayName": "displayName",
    "ssl": ""
  },
```

Cause of this issue is a change in the underlying `angus-mail` implementation, which was updated in Keycloak 22 to Angus-Mail:2.0.1. 
In jakarta.mail:1.6.5 the default for `mail.smtp.ssl.checkserveridentity` is `false`: 
https://github.com/jakartaee/mail-api/blob/1.6.5/mail/src/main/java/com/sun/mail/util/SocketFetcher.java#L608C46-L608C46
...whereas in angus.mail:2.0.1 the default is `true`: 
https://github.com/eclipse-ee4j/angus-mail/blob/master/core/src/main/java/org/eclipse/angus/mail/util/SocketFetcher.java#L634

However, the `DefaultEmailSenderProvider` was not updated to reflect this change. It would need to set this property explicitly for the case of `ANY` hostname verification
https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/main/services/src/main/java/org/keycloak/email/DefaultEmailSenderProvider.java#L195

For now, I have worked around the issue by providing my own `EmailSenderProvider` and setting this as the default.

### Version

22.0.1

### Expected behavior

Sending emails without hostname verification is possible. The default EmailSenderProvider instructs angus-mail to bypass the verification if so configured.

```java
private void setupTruststore(Properties props) {
        boolean checkServerIdentity = true;

        JSSETruststoreConfigurator configurator = new JSSETruststoreConfigurator(session);

        SSLSocketFactory factory = configurator.getSSLSocketFactory();
        if (factory != null) {
            props.put("mail.smtp.ssl.socketFactory", factory);
            if (configurator.getProvider().getPolicy() == HostnameVerificationPolicy.ANY) {
                props.setProperty("mail.smtp.ssl.trust", "*");
                props.setProperty("mail.smtp.ssl.checkserveridentity", "false"); // this is needed
                checkServerIdentity = false;
            }
        }

        if (checkServerIdentity) { 
            props.put("mail.smtp.ssl.checkserveridentity", "true"); // obsolete w/ angus-mail:2.0.1, but good to be explicit..?
        }
    }
```

### Actual behavior

Sending emails fails with the following error message:
```
"exception":{"refId":1,"exceptionType":"jakarta.mail.MessagingException",
    "message":"Could not convert socket to TLS","frames":
    [{"class":"org.eclipse.angus.mail.smtp.SMTPTransport","method":"startTLS","line":2188},
    {"class":"org.eclipse.angus.mail.smtp.SMTPTransport","method":"protocolConnect","line":765},
...
org.keycloak.services.resources.admin.RealmAdminResource: Failed to send email 
jakarta.mail.MessagingException: Could not convert socket to TLS;
nested exception is:
    java.io.IOException: Can't verify identity of server: our-mail-server.com
```

### How to Reproduce?

Set up keycloak with an SPI Truststore configured and try to send emails using StartTLS. Config example below:

Note.
I am unsure what needs to be configured to actually get an SSLFactory here...
```java
        JSSETruststoreConfigurator configurator = new JSSETruststoreConfigurator(session);

        SSLSocketFactory factory = configurator.getSSLSocketFactory();
```


keycloak.conf
```yaml
# Truststore
spi-truststore-file-enabled=true
spi-truststore-file-hostname-verification-policy=ANY
spi-truststore-file-password=very-secret
spi-truststore-file-file=/path/to/keycloak/truststore.jks
```

realm configuration:
```json
"smtpServer": {
    "starttls": "true",
    "port": "25",
    "auth": "",
    "host": "our-mail-server.com",
    "from": "we@domain.com",
    "fromDisplayName": "displayName",
    "ssl": ""
  },
```

### Anything else?

In jakarta.mail:1.6.5 the default for `mail.smtp.ssl.checkserveridentity` is `false`: 
https://github.com/jakartaee/mail-api/blob/1.6.5/mail/src/main/java/com/sun/mail/util/SocketFetcher.java#L608C46-L608C46
...whereas in angus.mail:2.0.1 the default is `true`: 
https://github.com/eclipse-ee4j/angus-mail/blob/master/core/src/main/java/org/eclipse/angus/mail/util/SocketFetcher.java#L634
commit_message:
Change email checkserveridentity prop as angus mail sets it to true by default
Closes https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/issues/22395

issue_title: Redirect URI with encoded curly brackets rejected
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have searched existing issues
- [X] I have reproduced the issue with the [latest nightly release](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/releases/tag/nightly)

### Area

core

### Describe the bug

The OpenID Connect Login is being refused, if the client redirect uri contains encoded curly brackets, even if they are correctly configured as valid redirect uri in keycloak client configuration.
This was working fine until keycloak 15.0.5 at least.

### Version

22.0.1

### Expected behavior

Keycloak displays the login form for the specified realm/client.

### Actual behavior

Error message:

> We are sorry...
Invalid parameter: redirect_uri

### How to Reproduce?

1. Configure a OpenID Connect Client with a redirect uri containing encoded curly braces, e.g. http://clienthost/%7B123%7D/
2. Add  http://clienthost/%7B123%7D/ as valid Request URI in keycloak client configuration.
3. Client will open this login request: https://keycloakhost/realms/.../protocol/openid-connect/auth?client_id=...&redirect_uri=http%3A%2F%2Flocalhost%2F%257B123%257D%2F&response_type=code&scope=openid&state=...&code_challenge=...&code_challenge_method=S256&response_mode=query

### Anything else?

- Could reproduce this in keycloak 20.0.5, 21.1.0 and 22.0.1.
- Maybe similar to #16851.
- When using redirect uri http://clienthost/123/ (without encoded curly brackets) it's working fine and the login form is being displayed as expected.
commit_message:
RedirectUtils needs to use KeycloakUriBuilder with no parameter parsing
Closes https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/issues/22424

issue_title: NPE when fetching sessions for a client in UI
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have searched existing issues
- [X] I have reproduced the issue with the [latest nightly release](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/releases/tag/nightly)

### Area

infinispan

### Describe the bug

Related to issue #20443, the solution is not complete.
The functions related to pagination of sessions for clients were not touched in the last PR.

Due to offline sessions for non-existing users (see discussion of PR #17577), the UI is not able to display anything.

### Version

22.0.1

### Expected behavior

The UI can show sessions of a client.

### Actual behavior

The UI is not showing sessions of a client when some of the sessions belong to a federated user which has been deleted just on the federated system.

### How to Reproduce?

1. Create an offline session for a federated user.
2. Delete the user from the external system without Keycloak getting informed about it.
3. Open the sessions view from the client details page of the Admin UI.

Then, after some time, the UI fails. In the browser debug window the request it can be observed that the request for offline sessions failed.

### Anything else?

This bug report is related to issue #20443 and PR #17577.
commit_message:
Reuse already fixed code to fetch offline user (#22429)

The problem is again the wrap(...) function.
In case the user is not found, then null is
returned. This can happen when a federated user
is deleted on the federation side but Keycloak
is not informed about it. In that case, the
session is still present but no UserModel can
be created.

Without this patch the stream contains null
values. Some downstream users can not cope well
with that.

The adjustment of the function getUserSessionsCount(...)
is slightly more expensive in execution, but
returns the correct number.

Closes #22428

Co-authored-by: Martin Krüger <mkrueger@mkru.de>

issue_title: Localization: Admin UI doesn't pick up message bundles from realms other than master
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have searched existing issues
- [X] I have reproduced the issue with the [latest nightly release](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/releases/tag/nightly)

### Area

admin/ui

### Describe the bug

I use the User Profile functionality and inside Admin UI -> Users -> User detail I should be able to provide translated names, but this only works for non-english locales.

### Version

22.0.1

### Expected behavior

I add a custom property in the User profile section with a translated name `${customAttribute}`, then I add a message bundle with the correct translations `users:customAttribute`. Then I should see my `users:customAttribute` value in the User details page.

### Actual behavior

I see `customAttribute` written there, but if I do the same procedure for eg. german, than it works!

### How to Reproduce?

1. Add a new custom attribute with name `customAttribute` in the user profile.
2. Add a displayname of `${customAttribute}`
3. Add a message bundle `users:customAttribute`
4. Look at a user profile

### Anything else?

As far as I can tell, there is a difference between how the english translation is handled and any other. 

After my procedure I see that the Admin UI is loading the translations from: http://localhost:8080/admin/realms/master/localization/en

But if I switch the language to german the translations are loaded from: http://localhost:8080/admin/realms/my-realm/localization/de

Or if I don't login through the master realm the english translations also work and are loaded from: http://localhost:8080/admin/realms/my-realm/localization/en

issue_title: inputOptionLabels is not used by Admin UI
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have searched existing issues
- [X] I have reproduced the issue with the [latest nightly release](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/releases/tag/nightly)

### Area

admin/ui

### Describe the bug

In the Admin UI the user profile option `inputOptionLabels`, which can be specified to provide translations to select values are not respected. If I specify them I get correctly translated forms in the login process but the keys are not translated in the admin ui.

### Version

22.0.1

### Expected behavior

With `inputOptionLabels` I should be able to provide translated labels for select options in the admin ui.

### Actual behavior

The `inputOptionLabels` option is ignored by admin ui.

### How to Reproduce?

1. Add a custom attribute in the user profile:
```json
{
      "name": "attr",
      "displayName": "${attr}",
      "selector": {
        "scopes": [
          "microprofile-jwt",
          "phone",
          "profile",
          "email",
          "web-origins",
          "roles",
          "offline_access",
          "role_list",
          "address",
          "acr"
        ]
      },
      "permissions": {
        "edit": [
          "user",
          "admin"
        ],
        "view": [
          "user",
          "admin"
        ]
      },
      "annotations": {
        "inputType": "select",
        "inputOptionLabels": {
          "option1": "${option1Label}",
          "option2": "${option2Label}",
        }
      },
      "validations": {
        "options": {
          "options": [
            "option1",
            "option2"
          ]
        }
      },
      "required": {
        "roles": [
          "user"
        ],
        "scopes": [
          "microprofile-jwt",
          "phone",
          "profile",
          "email",
          "web-origins",
          "roles",
          "offline_access",
          "role_list",
          "address",
          "acr"
        ]
      },
      "group": null
    },
```
2. Check the User detail page of a user
3. It says `option1` and `option2` instead of `option1Label` and `option2Label`

### Anything else?

_No response_

issue_title: Incorrect logout from GitHub
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have searched existing issues
- [X] I have reproduced the issue with the [latest nightly release](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/releases/tag/nightly)

### Area

testsuite

### Describe the bug

The `SocialLoginTest.githubLogin` and `SocialLoginTest.githubPrivateEmailLogin` test cases incorrectly log out when the user is already logged out.

### Version

latest

### Expected behavior

The test logouts before the login process to ensure that the user is not logged in.

Using **non-headless** mode it should show this page first
`https://github.com/logout`
![image](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/assets/67582554/30dddf88-01d3-4a53-9145-49288690003e)

and after clicking on the "Sign out" button, this one
`https://github.com`
![image](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/assets/67582554/958a57ab-b086-44b5-b8ce-dee3539637f8)

where the login process is ready to start...


### Actual behavior

When the test tries to logout (i.e., redirects to `https://github.com/logout`) and the user is already logged out, it redirects to `https://github.com` and then it tries to click on the "Sign out" button, which is not there and therefore the test fails with:

`NoSuchElement no such element: Unable to locate element: {"method":"xpath","selector":"//input[@type='submit']"}`

### How to Reproduce?

1. Set up the GitHub application according to the documentation https://www.keycloak.org/docs/latest/server_admin/index.html#_github
2. Prepare `social.properties` file containing the required variables (e.g., Client ID, Client Secret, ...)
3. Run the testsuite with following command with your custom `social.properties` file
```
$ mvn -f testsuite/integration-arquillian/pom.xml clean install \
      -Dtest=SocialLoginTest#githubLogin \
      -Dsocial.config=path\to\social.properties \
      -Dbrowser=chrome
```


### Anything else?

_No response_

Handle GitHub logout properly (#22463)

Add profile info update to GitHub login test cases

Closes #22461

Signed-off-by: Tomas Ondrusko <tondrusk@redhat.com>

issue_title: Custom User Provider SPI: MULTIVALUED_STRING_TYPE value isn't correctly displayed on ui (but correctly saved and retrieved)
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have searched existing issues
- [X] I have reproduced the issue with the [latest nightly release](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/releases/tag/nightly)

### Area

admin/ui

### Describe the bug

The Custom User Provider UI doesn't displayed MULTIVALUED_STRING_TYPE setting correctly.

Same bug closed here: https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/issues/17937

The problem is not present in version 21.0.1 but present in version 21.1.2 and 22.0.1.
After analysis, the major problem between these versions is the way the data of the MULTIVALUED_STRING_TYPE is fetched from the UI.

First of all, we have to know:
- In version 21.0.1, the data is saved in a String format:
```
"my-config": ["CONF1##CONF2##CONF3"]
```
- In version 21.1.2 and 22.0.1; the data is saved in a Array format:
```
"my-config": [
  "CONF1",
  "CONF2",
  "CONF3"
]
```

In all these versions, it's seems that the data is still fetched in a String format.

Steps of the analysis :

1. https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/22.0.1/js/apps/admin-ui/src/user-federation/custom/CustomProviderSettings.tsx
When fetch of UserFederation's data is made from `CustomProviderSettings.tsx`, it uses the `convertToFormValues` function.

2. https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/22.0.1/js/apps/admin-ui/src/util.ts
This `convertToFormValues` function enters in the `if (key === "config" || key === "attributes") {` condition and takes only the first list element (tested in browser debug mode):
```
const convertedValues = Object.entries(flattened).map(([key, value]) =>
  Array.isArray(value) ? [key, value[0]] : [key, value]
);
```
In our example, the `convertedValues` takes only the first element of our config: `convertedValues = ["my-config", "CONF1"]`. Only the first element is passed to `setValue`.

3. https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/22.0.1/js/apps/admin-ui/src/user-federation/custom/CustomProviderSettings.tsx
The called component is `DynamicComponents`.

4. https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/22.0.1/js/apps/admin-ui/src/components/dynamic/DynamicComponents.tsx
The next called is `Component`, which called the `MultivaluedStringComponent` because of the configuration.

5. https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/22.0.1/js/apps/admin-ui/src/components/dynamic/MultivaluedStringComponent.tsx
The next one is `MultiLineInput`.

6. https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/22.0.1/js/apps/admin-ui/src/components/multi-line-input/MultiLineInput.tsx
In the `MultiLineInput` Component, `stringify` is set to `false` (because of the call hierarchy, verified in debug mode).
The `fields` to set in the UI are taken from this fragment of code:
```
const fields = useMemo<string[]>(() => {
  let values = stringify ? stringToMultiline(value as string) : value;

  values =
    Array.isArray(values) && values.length !== 0
	  ? values
	  : defaultValue || [""];

  return values;
}, [value]);
```
Here is the function `stringToMultiline`:
```
function stringToMultiline(value?: string): string[] {
  return typeof value === "string" ? value.split("##") : [];
}
```
`values` takes `value`, which is `"CONF1"`.
`values` is not an array, so `values` takes the `defaultValue || [""]`.
In my configuration, defaultValue is displayed each time I try to go the configuration page of my UserFederation's config.

To correct this, in my opinion:
- It's necessary to change the `convertToFormValues` function to return an array instead of the first string of the array (step 2).
or
- It's necessary to change the way the data is saved or returned by the API to return a String with the '##' separator and set the `stringify` boolean to `true`.

issue_title: Remove Brotli dependencies from the distribution and operator
issue_body:
### Analysis:

* Brotli is a compress algorithm - https://github.com/hyperxpro/Brotli4j

* Quarkus Platform BOM 3.2.3 contains two dependencies with the `com.aayushatharva.brotli4j` group ID:
```
       <dependency>
        <groupId>com.aayushatharva.brotli4j</groupId>
        <artifactId>brotli4j</artifactId>
        <version>1.12.0</version>
      </dependency>
      <dependency>
        <groupId>com.aayushatharva.brotli4j</groupId>
        <artifactId>native-linux-x86_64</artifactId>
        <version>1.12.0</version>
      </dependency>
```
https://github.com/quarkusio/quarkus-platform/blob/3.2.3.Final/generated-platform-project/quarkus/bom/pom.xml#L78-L88

* Quarkus seems to use brotli4j for its Netty extension, which is a part of the Quarkus foundation layer. They seem to override the dependency to an older version due to:
```
The recent version of Netty have a hard dependency on brotli, without this dependency, it's not possible to compile to native
```
https://github.com/quarkusio/quarkus/blob/3.2.3.Final/extensions/netty/runtime/pom.xml#L65-L74

* the Keycloak dependency tree output confirms the same findings:
```
Dependency tree for com.aayushatharva.brotli4j:brotli4j

         \- com.aayushatharva.brotli4j:brotli4j:jar:1.12.0:compile
org.keycloak:keycloak-guides:pom:999.0.0-SNAPSHOT
\- org.keycloak:keycloak-guides-maven-plugin:jar:999.0.0-SNAPSHOT:compile
   \- org.keycloak:keycloak-quarkus-server:jar:999.0.0-SNAPSHOT:compile

org.keycloak:keycloak-quarkus-server-deployment:jar:999.0.0-SNAPSHOT
\- org.keycloak:keycloak-quarkus-server:jar:999.0.0-SNAPSHOT:compile

      \- io.quarkus:quarkus-netty:jar:3.2.3.Final:compile

org.keycloak:keycloak-quarkus-server-app:jar:999.0.0-SNAPSHOT

org.keycloak:keycloak-junit5:jar:999.0.0-SNAPSHOT
\- org.keycloak:keycloak-quarkus-server:jar:999.0.0-SNAPSHOT:compile

==========================================================================
Dependency tree for com.aayushatharva.brotli4j:native-linux-x86_64
---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
```
where `netty/vertex` seems to be transitively dependant on `brotli4j` and the second of the dependencies `native-linux-x86_64` does not seem to be used anywhere.

On top of that, operator's dependency tree also contains `brotli4j` dependecy:
```
[INFO] --- dependency:3.1.2:tree (default-cli) @ keycloak-operator ---
[INFO] Verbose not supported since maven-dependency-plugin 3.0
[INFO] org.keycloak:keycloak-operator:jar:999.0.0-SNAPSHOT
[INFO] \- io.quarkus:quarkus-kubernetes-client:jar:3.2.3.Final:compile
[INFO]    \- io.quarkus:quarkus-vertx:jar:3.2.3.Final:compile
[INFO]       \- io.quarkus:quarkus-netty:jar:3.2.3.Final:compile
[INFO]          \- com.aayushatharva.brotli4j:brotli4j:jar:1.12.0:compile
```

After a following discussion it's clear that Brotli (as an alternative to GZip) is not going to be supported by Keycloak. Therefore, the dependencies can be excluded.

issue_title: Timing issue with running operator tests
issue_body:
### Description

A test https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/actions/runs/5881673215/job/15950974936?pr=22502 failed with: io.javaoperatorsdk.operator.OperatorException: Error starting operator

The cause was: 

io.fabric8.kubernetes.client.KubernetesClientException: Failure executing: GET at: https://192.168.49.2:8443/apis/k8s.keycloak.org/v2alpha1/namespaces/keycloak-test-d003525d-508e-4b9b-a9df-1f5f5000bbb9/keycloakrealmimports?resourceVersion=0. Message: Not Found.

Which is from the initial list performed by the operator.  This means that the CRDs, though installed, had not yet become ready for use (we have seen similar issues with fabric8 testing).  There is more that we can do to detect that the CRDs are ready for use by inspecting the api-server metadata, or the operator should be configurable to tolerate startup issues like this.

issue_title: Description of "Configuring sources for Keycloak" inconsistent / misleading
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have searched existing issues
- [X] I have reproduced the issue with the [latest nightly release](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/releases/tag/nightly)

### Area

docs

### Describe the bug

The section "Configuring sources for Keycloak" in https://www.keycloak.org/server/configuration talks about "four sources of configuration", but then lists five:

> - Command-line parameters
> - Environment variables
> - Options defined in a user-created configuration file.
> - Options defined in the `conf/keycloak.conf` file.
> - Sensitive options defined in a user-created Java KeyStore file.

This is inconsistent and a bit misleading - when a user-created configuration file is used, `conf/keycloak.conf` is not taken into account anymore. The description in the later section "Format to include a specific configuration file" also does not unambiguously tell that a user-created configuration file is *replacing* `conf/keycloak.conf`.

### Version

22

### Expected behavior

The section "Configuring sources for Keycloak" in https://www.keycloak.org/server/configuration should unambigously describe the current behaviour of the software ("Options defined in a user-created configuration file or in the `conf/keycloak.conf` file."?)

### Actual behavior

The section is inconsistent and somewhat misleading (see "Describe the bug").

### How to Reproduce?

Look at the section "Configuring sources for Keycloak" in https://www.keycloak.org/server/configuration.

### Anything else?

Side-note: I would rather prefer if Keycloak could actually use a user-defined configuration file *in addition to* the default one (after my first read of the documentation I thought that would be possible).

issue_title: idp jwt userinfo broken
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have reproduced the issue with the [latest nightly release](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/releases/tag/nightly)

### Area

identity-brokering

### Describe the bug

When the userinfo endpoint is a JWT, the accesstoken is passed to the parseTokenInput instead of the userinfo response string

This bug was introduced here:
https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/commit/09e30b3c992e4d6d2b73fb90794b6c960136867a

This line:
https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/22.0.1/services/src/main/java/org/keycloak/broker/oidc/OIDCIdentityProvider.java#L495

Should pass response.asString() to the parseTokenInput method instead of accessToken


`userInfo = JsonSerialization.readValue(parseTokenInput(accessToken, false), JsonNode.class);`
should be:
`userInfo = JsonSerialization.readValue(parseTokenInput(response.asString(), false), JsonNode.class);`


Link above references 22.0.1, but is currently still present in the last commit on the main branch:
https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/c060ae094b8c1af7753a63d06f2f9478225ce651/services/src/main/java/org/keycloak/broker/oidc/OIDCIdentityProvider.java#L499




### Version

22.0.1

### Expected behavior

idp userinfo request works

### Actual behavior

idp userinfo request fails

### How to Reproduce?

set up an idp in keycloak and enable the userinfo endpoint

Important: use an idp that does not use jwt tokens for its access tokens, else it might seem to work

Try to log in using that idp -> it will fail

### Anything else?

issue_title: Update the Keycloak SAML adapter subsystem to no longer use the AttributeDefinition#getAttributeMarshaller method
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have searched existing issues
- [X] I have reproduced the issue with the [latest nightly release](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/releases/tag/nightly)

### Area

adapter/jee-saml

### Describe the bug

The Keycloak SAML adapter subsystem currently references the `AttributeDefinition#getAttributeMarshaller` method that has been removed in WildFly Core 21.1.0.Final and later.

This means that attempting to use the `keycloak-saml-adapter-galleon-pack` with WildFly 29 and later results in the following exception when attempting to add subsystem resources:

```
 java.lang.NoSuchMethodError: 'org.jboss.as.controller.AttributeMarshaller org.jboss.as.controller.SimpleAttributeDefinition.getAttributeMarshaller()'
at org.keycloak.keycloak-saml-wildfly-jakarta-subsystem@22.0.1//org.keycloak.subsystem.adapter.saml.extension.KeycloakSubsystemParser.writeSps(KeycloakSubsystemParser.java:501)
at org.keycloak.keycloak-saml-wildfly-jakarta-subsystem@22.0.1//org.keycloak.subsystem.adapter.saml.extension.KeycloakSubsystemParser.writeSecureDeployment(KeycloakSubsystemParser.java:487)
at org.keycloak.keycloak-saml-wildfly-jakarta-subsystem@22.0.1//org.keycloak.subsystem.adapter.saml.extension.KeycloakSubsystemParser.writeContent(KeycloakSubsystemParser.java:474)
```
`KeycloakSubsystemParser` needs to be updated to make use of the `AttributeDefinition#getMarshaller` method instead.




### Version

22.0.1

### Expected behavior

 It should be possible to successfully use the `keycloak-saml-adapter-galleon-pack` with WildFly 29 and later. 

### Actual behavior

 It's currently not possible to use the `keycloak-saml-adapter-galleon-pack` with WildFly 29 and later when specifying the configuration required to secure SAML applications via the Keycloak SAML adapter subsystem.

### How to Reproduce?

Using `galleon.sh`, attempt to provision a WildFly 29 server using the Keycloak SAML adapter feature pack:

```
galleon.sh install wildfly:current --layers=web-server,core-tools --dir=wildfly
galleon.sh install org.keycloak:keycloak-saml-adapter-galleon-pack:22.0.1 --layers=keycloak-client-saml --dir=wildfly
```

Execute the following WildFly CLI commands:

```
/subsystem=keycloak-saml/secure-deployment=saml-app.war:add()
/subsystem=keycloak-saml/secure-deployment=saml-app.war/SP=saml-app:add(sslPolicy=EXTERNAL)
```

The following error will occur:

```
 java.lang.NoSuchMethodError: 'org.jboss.as.controller.AttributeMarshaller org.jboss.as.controller.SimpleAttributeDefinition.getAttributeMarshaller()'
at org.keycloak.keycloak-saml-wildfly-jakarta-subsystem@22.0.1//org.keycloak.subsystem.adapter.saml.extension.KeycloakSubsystemParser.writeSps(KeycloakSubsystemParser.java:501)
at org.keycloak.keycloak-saml-wildfly-jakarta-subsystem@22.0.1//org.keycloak.subsystem.adapter.saml.extension.KeycloakSubsystemParser.writeSecureDeployment(KeycloakSubsystemParser.java:487)
at org.keycloak.keycloak-saml-wildfly-jakarta-subsystem@22.0.1//org.keycloak.subsystem.adapter.saml.extension.KeycloakSubsystemParser.writeContent(KeycloakSubsystemParser.java:474)
```

### Anything else?

I will submit a PR to fix this.

main

not failing test

failing test

issue_title: performance regression with running the local tests after realm import dependent resource conversion
issue_body:
There appears to be a performance regression with running the local tests: https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/actions/runs/5927179684/job/16070891213?pr=22299

It may be related to the additional overhead of logging the full realm secret into the console log that is captured by ci - as the remote tests do not seem to have the same issue.  If this does turn out to be the problem, it has already been addressed by upstream changes in the operator sdk.

_Originally posted by @shawkins in https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/issues/22223#issuecomment-1687282963_
commit_message:
removes the SSA logging that is capturing the entire resource (#22608)

Closes #22607

issue_title: SAML AdvancedAttributeToRoleMapper does not allow predicate evaluation on same Array Attribute
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have searched existing issues
- [X] I have reproduced the issue with the [latest nightly release](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/releases/tag/nightly)

### Area

saml

### Describe the bug

![d3850847-10cc-464b-81a5-688daa2a5805](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/assets/15670023/2f576c5c-f01e-4db4-8d1e-6a2afbaf7d91)
```
<saml:Attribute xmlns:xs="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema" xmlns:xsi="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance" Name="groupMembership" NameFormat="urn:oasis:names:tc:SAML:2.0:attrname-format:unspecified">
	<saml:AttributeValue xsi:type="xs:string">cn=captain,ou=...,ou=...,o=...</saml:AttributeValue>
	<saml:AttributeValue xsi:type="xs:string">cn=america,ou=...,ou=...,o=...</saml:AttributeValue>
```

Configuration above leads to an "duplicate key groupMembership..." error in Keycloak's log and no mapping at all.

### Version

22.0.1

### Expected behavior

I expect following behaviour (for the example above):

_if I am a group member of "america" and a group member of "captain" give  me the role "manager_us"_

### Actual behavior

Predicates (equals or regex) on different keys evaluate with AND.
Predicates on same key evaluate not at all.

### How to Reproduce?

Define predicates on same key.

### Anything else?

I consider this a bug because of the inconsistent behavior and the inability to express a common use-case.

Fix should be easy when I look into the source code.

### Area

testsuite

### Describe the bug

There is an additional dialog while logging into Keycloak using Microsoft account.

```
      -Dtest=SocialLoginTest#microsoftLogin \
      -Dsocial.config=path\to\social.properties \
      -Dbrowser=chrome
```

issue_title: ClientIdAndSecretCredentialsProvider throws NPE
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have searched existing issues
- [X] I have reproduced the issue with the [latest nightly release](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/releases/tag/nightly)

### Area

adapter/jee

### Describe the bug

I am using the `keycloak-servlet-filter-adapter` and since version 21.1.0 it throws a NullPointerException (see below). The last working version is 21.0.2. 

```
java.lang.NullPointerException: null
        at org.keycloak.protocol.oidc.client.authentication.ClientIdAndSecretCredentialsProvider.setClientCredentials(ClientIdAndSecretCredentialsProvider.java:53)
        at org.keycloak.protocol.oidc.client.authentication.ClientCredentialsProviderUtils.setClientCredentials(ClientCredentialsProviderUtils.java:91)
        at org.keycloak.adapters.AdapterUtils.setClientCredentials(AdapterUtils.java:106)
        at org.keycloak.adapters.ServerRequest.invokeAccessCodeToToken(ServerRequest.java:106)
        at org.keycloak.adapters.OAuthRequestAuthenticator.resolveCode(OAuthRequestAuthenticator.java:335)
        at org.keycloak.adapters.OAuthRequestAuthenticator.authenticate(OAuthRequestAuthenticator.java:280)
        at org.keycloak.adapters.RequestAuthenticator.authenticate(RequestAuthenticator.java:138)
        at org.keycloak.adapters.servlet.KeycloakOIDCFilter.doFilter(KeycloakOIDCFilter.java:204)
        [....]
```

I looked in the source code and found that `DeploymentDelegate` within `AdapterDeploymentContext` does not overwrite `getAdapterConfig()`, and returns `null` instead (while the comment on the class says "Ever method is delegated except URL get methods and isConfigured()"). Is this on purpose? Or am I missing something here?

### Version

since 21.1.0 (also with 22.0.1)

### Expected behavior

No NPE is thrown.

### Actual behavior

NPE is thrown

### How to Reproduce?

Configure a  `keycloak-servlet-filter-adapter` and use the `KeycloakOIDCFilter`

Our configuration looks as below

```
{
  "realm": "master",
  "auth-server-url" : "/auth",
  "ssl-required": "external",
  "resource": "<resource-name>",
  "credentials": {
    "secret": "<secret>"
  },
  "confidential-port": 0
}
```

### Anything else?

_No response_

Fix NPE by calling delegate in DeploymentDelegate.getAdapterConfig()

issue_title: Missing metrics for worker_pool_*
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have searched existing issues
- [X] I have reproduced the issue with the [latest nightly release](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/releases/tag/nightly)

### Area

dist/quarkus

### Describe the bug

There are no metrics on the execution of the RESTEasy requests, as the executor pool is called directly.

### Version

999-SNAPSHOT

### Expected behavior

As suggested in https://github.com/quarkusio/quarkus/issues/34998, the Vert.x pool metrics will be populated when calling `vertx().executeBlocking()`. 



### Actual behavior

Metrics are not recorded for the executor pool, including active threads, queue size and queue waiting time.

### How to Reproduce?

* Start Keycloak in production mode with metrics: 
   ```
   bin/kc.sh --verbose start --http-enabled true --hostname-strict false  --metrics-enabled true --health-enabled true
   ```
* Perform some activities (like logging in)
* Look at `/metrics` and metric `worker_pool_completed_total` - before the change the value is 0, after the PR it is >0

### Anything else?

I'll prepare a pull request
commit_message:
Avoid direct access to executor to record worker pool metrics

Closes #22742

issue_title: Deep link format for redirect uri parameter is not parsed correctly
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have searched existing issues
- [X] I have reproduced the issue with the [latest nightly release](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/releases/tag/nightly)

### Area

core

### Describe the bug

Hi!
We have noticed that "redirect uri" parameter is no longer parsed correctly if it's in "deep link" format, example: cell.sso://oauth2redirect

We have an "*" in client settings for "Valid redirect URIs" parameter but still getting an error in log:
2023-08-28 14:44:32,444 WARN  [org.keycloak.events] (executor-thread-2) type=LOGIN_ERROR, realmId=cell, clientId=my-cell-app-android, userId=null, ipAddress=x.x.x.x, error=invalid_redirect_uri, redirect_uri=cell.sso://oauth2redirect

Update: DEBUG mode shows:
2023-08-29 14:38:08,309 DEBUG [org.keycloak.protocol.oidc.utils.RedirectUtils] (executor-thread-32) Invalid URI **because scheme is not allowed**: cell.sso://oauth2redirect

Setting "Valid redirect URIs" parameter to exact value cell.sso://oauth2redirect along with "*" seems to fix this issue but logout flow not working still:
2023-08-28 17:35:32,825 ERROR [org.keycloak.services.error.KeycloakErrorHandler] (executor-thread-67) Uncaught server error: java.lang.NullPointerException: Cannot invoke "String.startsWith(String)" because "redirectUri" is null
	at org.keycloak.protocol.oidc.utils.RedirectUtils.verifyRedirectUri(RedirectUtils.java:146)
	at org.keycloak.protocol.oidc.utils.RedirectUtils.verifyRealmRedirectUri(RedirectUtils.java:54)
	at org.keycloak.protocol.oidc.endpoints.LogoutEndpoint.logout(LogoutEndpoint.java:245)
	at jdk.internal.reflect.GeneratedMethodAccessor526.invoke(Unknown Source)
	at java.base/jdk.internal.reflect.DelegatingMethodAccessorImpl.invoke(DelegatingMethodAccessorImpl.java:43
	
Logout url looks as
https://sso-test.cell.com.ua/auth/realms/cell/protocol/openid-connect/logout?redirect_uri=https://sso-test.cell.com.ua/auth/realms/cell/protocol/openid-connect/auth?redirect_uri=cell.sso://oauth2redirect&client_id=my-cell-app-android&response_type=code&ui_locales=uk&state=4gymDiO8wvpF3Gz0d0DaQw&nonce=8o8NHt7CIKVsVo1u8giKzQ&scope=openid offline_access&code_challenge=gOu6ZP8Lb4nICu3tqoami2RmCaj91cGA7lP9J4fvox4&code_challenge_method=S256

with redirect_uri parameter in deep link format, but it seems like keycloak is not sees it.

 

### Version

22.0.1

### Expected behavior

"redirect uri" parameter is parsed correctly

### Actual behavior

2023-08-28 14:44:32,444 WARN  [org.keycloak.events] (executor-thread-2) type=LOGIN_ERROR, realmId=cell, clientId=my-cell-app-android, userId=null, ipAddress=x.x.x.x, error=invalid_redirect_uri, redirect_uri=cell.sso://oauth2redirect

### How to Reproduce?

Create client with "Valid redirect URIs" parameter in deep link format.

### Anything else?

_No response_
commit_message:
verifyRedirectUri should return null when the passed redirectUri is invalid
Closes https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/issues/22778
